text.title: Tapioca RBI Pipeline for Rails DSLs and Gems
text.summary: Tapioca runtime-introspects gems and Rails DSLs to emit static RBIs that Sorbet reads, closing the metaprogramming gap.

section.title: Generate, commit, and verify RBIs
section.p: Sorbet sees only static Ruby; Rails relies on metaprogramming (`has_many`, `scope`, attribute readers from columns). Tapioca calls into the running app and emits `.rbi` files describing those generated methods so Sorbet can typecheck call sites against the real shape. The RBIs MUST be committed; CI re-runs `tapioca check-shims` to detect drift between checked-in RBIs and the current code.

rule.statement: Every project ships `bin/tapioca`; the workflow is `tapioca init` once, then `tapioca gem` after `Gemfile.lock` changes, `tapioca dsl` after model/concern changes. RBIs (`sorbet/rbi/gems/*.rbi`, `sorbet/rbi/dsl/*.rbi`) MUST be committed. CI MUST run `bundle exec tapioca check-shims` AND `bundle exec tapioca dsl --verify` — any divergence fails the build.
rule.rationale: Without `check-shims` and `dsl --verify`, an agent can edit code that invalidates a checked-in RBI, get a green local typecheck against the stale RBI, and ship a method call that breaks at boot. The verify pass is the determinism guarantee.

example.code:
# After adding a new gem bundle add stripe bin/tapioca gem stripe git add sorbet/rbi/gems/stripe@*.rbi Gemfile Gemfile.lock git commit -m "deps: add stripe + tapioca rbi"
# After changing an ActiveRecord model bin/tapioca dsl User git add sorbet/rbi/dsl/user.rbi
example.note: Each shape change is paired with the matching RBI commit.

example.code: # Agent adds a gem and skips the RBI step bundle add stripe git add Gemfile Gemfile.lock git commit -m "feat: add stripe billing" # srb tc passes (stripe types unknown, falls back to T.untyped) # Production: NoMethodError on the first Stripe::Charge call.
example.note: Without RBI, Sorbet can't refute hallucinated Stripe APIs.

antipattern.description: Edit a hand-written shim under `sorbet/rbi/shims/` to silence a Sorbet error instead of regenerating the DSL RBI.
antipattern.reason: Hand-written shims lie about the real shape; `tapioca check-shims` flags them, but if CI is missing the check, drift compounds silently.

section.reference: Tapioca config with DSL compilers and gem skiplist.
